title: ZOE Marinated Olives with Greek Yoghurt
summary: Got friends coming over? This plate is a great way to kick things off. Olives are packed with monounsaturated fatty acids including elenolic acid, which has been shown to lower blood sugar levels and promote weight loss. Olives are also a great source of polyphenols, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote better gut health. We’ve marinated our olives using Daily 30+ which adds so much flavour as well as 5g of fibre per scoop from over 30 different plants. The Greek yoghurt is a source of probiotics making this a feast for you and your gut microbes.

method.description: Add the drained olives, Daily30+, olive oil and herbs to a bowl and stir to combine. Set aside – you can do this step a few days in advance to increase the flavour.
Stir a pinch of salt through the greek yoghurt. Spread onto a plate and top with the marinated olives.
Serve with toasted sourdough and enjoy!

chefs.name: Healthy by ZOE
chefs.summary: ZOE (@ZOE) is a science and nutrition company leading a movement to transform the health of millions through smarter food choices. ZOE means life in Greek — and ZOE passionately believes everyone can feel better and live longer, healthier lives by rethinking what they eat. ZOE conducts the world’s largest nutrition science study to better understand the relationship between food and health. Based on this research, their personalised nutrition program delivers tailored advice based on metabolic and gut health, turning complex science into clear step-by-step actions to improve your health with every meal. Become a member at zoe.com. All of their recipes are developed by nutritionists to help you embrace food that tastes amazing and makes you feel even better.
